vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe biological collapse. The registry-defined specimens are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (String of Nickels): Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears empty.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Mexican Mint): Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears empty.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Pothos): Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent or completely desiccated.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil surface shows no plant matter.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and appears saturated or potentially hydrophobic due to lack of organic uptake.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit anchor (P3) is the only non-soil object. No fungal blooms or incidental growth detected.
biome_observations.surface_conditions: No signs of recent growth or biological activity.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Across the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of plant growth, recovery, or change. The environment is static and devoid of living botanical specimens.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. All registered plants have suffered total mortality or have been removed from the pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an otherwise empty pot (P3) is the only structural feature. No new introductions or interventions observed.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the spatial orientation of the pots and identify the anchor point (the white rabbit). Upon validation, I compared the visual data against the registry. The conclusion is definitive: the biome has suffered a total systemic loss of all four registered plant species. The pots contain only soil and the anchor. There is no evidence of life, growth, or recent maintenance.
